_meta.description: Phase 2 data patch for gladness registry 186: 7 new mti_terms, wa_file_index, wa_term_inventory, verse records, quality flags, root families, and related words.

rows.meaning: to be cheerful, glad; to be animated, encouraged, in good spirits
rows.lsj_entry: to be of good courage; farewell! on tomb inscriptions; = chairein in condolence letters

rows.meaning: to be good, be pleasing, be well, be glad; Qal: be glad/joyful, be well placed, be pleasing; Hiphil: make glad/rejoice, do good to, do well, make beautiful

rows.occurrence_count_qualifier: same verses as ya.tav (H3190); Aramaic equivalent
rows.meaning: Aramaic equivalent of ya.tav (H3190): to be glad, be good (P'al)
act.: to cause celebration, make glad; mid./pass.: to celebrate, rejoice, be glad
rows.lsj_entry: cheer, gladden; pass.: make merry, enjoy oneself

rows.meaning: to rejoice; Qal: to rejoice; Piel: to make glad, gladden
welfare, benefit, good things; prosperity/happiness; good things (collective); bounty

rows.verse_text: I hope in the Lord Jesus to send Timothy to you soon, so that I too may be cheered by news of you.
If you do well, will you not be accepted? And if you do not do well, sin is crouching at the door. Its desire is for you, and you must rule over it.”
Say you are my sister, that it may go well with me because of you, and that my life may be spared for your sake.”
And for her sake he dealt well with Abram; and he had sheep, oxen, male donkeys, male servants, female servants, female donkeys, and camels.
And Jacob said, “O God of my father Abraham and God of my father Isaac, O LORD who said to me, ‘Return to your country and to your kindred, that I may do you good,’
But you said, ‘I will surely do you good, and make your offspring as the sand of the sea, which cannot be numbered for multitude.’”
Their words pleased Hamor and Hamor’s son Shechem.
Only remember me, when it is well with you, and please do me the kindness to mention me to Pharaoh, and so get me out of this house.
This proposal pleased Pharaoh and all his servants.
When the report was heard in Pharaoh’s house, “Joseph’s brothers have come,” it pleased Pharaoh and his servants.
So God dealt well with the midwives. And the people multiplied and grew very strong.
And Aaron shall burn fragrant incense on it. Every morning when he dresses the lamps he shall burn it,

or if anyone utters with his lips a rash oath to do evil or to do good, any sort of rash oath that people swear, and it is hidden from him, when he comes to know it, and he realizes his guilt in any of these;

And Aaron said to Moses, “Behold, today they have offered their sin offering and their burnt offering before the LORD, and yet such things as these have happened to me! If I had eaten the sin offering today, would the LORD have approved?”
And when Moses heard that, he approved.

And Moses said to Hobab the son of Reuel the Midianite, Moses’ father-in-law, “We are setting out for the place of which the LORD said, ‘I will give it to you.’ Come with us, and we will do good to you, for the LORD has promised good to Israel.”
And if you do go with us, whatever good the LORD will do to us, the same will we do to you.”
The thing seemed good to me, and I took twelve men from you, one man from each tribe.

Therefore you shall keep his statutes and his commandments, which I command you today, that it may go well with you and with your children after you, and that you may prolong your days in the land that the LORD your God is giving you for all time.”
“‘Honor your father and your mother, as the LORD your God commanded you, that your days may be long, and that it may go well with you in the land that the LORD your God is giving you.
“And the LORD heard your words, when you spoke to me. And the LORD said to me, ‘I have heard the words of this people, which they have spoken to you. They are right in all that they have spoken.
Oh that they had such a heart as this always, to fear me and to keep all my commandments, that it might go well with them and with their descendants forever!

Hear therefore, O Israel, and be careful to do them, that it may go well with you, and that you may multiply greatly, as the LORD, the God of your fathers, has promised you, in a land flowing with milk and honey.
And you shall do what is right and good in the sight of the LORD, that it may go well with you, and that you may go in and take possession of the good land that the LORD swore to give to your fathers
who fed you in the wilderness with manna that your fathers did not know, that he might humble you and test you, to do you good in the end.

Then I took the sinful thing, the calf that you had made, and burned it with fire and crushed it, grinding it very small, until it was as fine as dust. And I threw the dust of it into the brook that ran down from the mountain.
You shall not eat it, that all may go well with you and with your children after you, when you do what is right in the sight of the LORD.
Be careful to obey all these words that I command you, that it may go well with you and with your children after you forever, when you do what is good and right in the sight of the LORD your God.
then you shall inquire and make search and ask diligently. And behold, if it be true and certain that such an abomination has been done among you,
and it is told you and you hear of it, then you shall inquire diligently, and if it is true and certain that such an abomination has been done in Israel,
And the LORD said to me, ‘They are right in what they have spoken.
The judges shall inquire diligently, and if the witness is a false witness and has accused his brother falsely,
You shall let the mother go, but the young you may take for yourself, that it may go well with you, and that you may live long.
And you shall write on the stones all the words of this law very plainly.”

And as the LORD took delight in doing you good and multiplying you, so the LORD will take delight in bringing ruin upon you and destroying you. And you shall be plucked off the land that you are entering to take possession of it.
And the LORD your God will bring you into the land that your fathers possessed, that you may possess it. And he will make you more prosperous and numerous than your fathers.

When Phinehas the priest and the chiefs of the congregation, the heads of the families of Israel who were with him, heard the words that the people of Reuben and the people of Gad and the people of Manasseh spoke, it was good in their eyes.

And the report was good in the eyes of the people of Israel. And the people of Israel blessed God and spoke no more of making war against them to destroy the land where the people of Reuben and the people of Gad were settled.
If you forsake the LORD and serve foreign gods, then he will turn and do you harm and consume you, after having done you good.”
Then Micah said, “Now I know that the LORD will prosper me, because I have a Levite as priest.”
And the priest’s heart was glad. He took the ephod and the household gods and the carved image and went along with the people.
So the two of them sat and ate and drank together. And the girl’s father said to the man, “Be pleased to spend the night, and let your heart be merry.”

And when the man and his concubine and his servant rose up to depart, his father-in-law, the girl’s father, said to him, “Behold, now the day has waned toward evening. Please, spend the night. Behold, the day draws to its close. Lodge here and let your heart be merry, and tomorrow you shall arise early in the morning for your journey, and go home.”

As they were making their hearts merry, behold, the men of the city, worthless fellows, surrounded the house, beating on the door. And they said to the old man, the master of the house, “Bring out the man who came into your house, that we may know him.”
Then Naomi her mother-in-law said to her, “My daughter, should I not seek rest for you, that it may be well with you?
And when Boaz had eaten and drunk, and his heart was merry, he went to lie down at the end of the heap of grain. Then she came softly and uncovered his feet and lay down.
And he said, “May you be blessed by the LORD, my daughter. You have made this last kindness greater than the first in that you have not gone after young men, whether poor or rich.
Whatever seems good to you and your brothers to do with the rest of the silver and gold, you may do, according to the will of your God.
And I said to the king, “If it pleases the king, and if your servant has found favor in your sight, that you send me to Judah, to the city of my fathers’ graves, that I may rebuild it.”
And the king said to me ( the queen sitting beside him), “How long will you be gone, and when will you return?” So it pleased the king to send me when I had given him a time.
This advice pleased the king and the princes, and the king did as Memucan proposed.
And let the young woman who pleases the king be queen instead of Vashti.” This pleased the king, and he did so.

And the young woman pleased him and won his favor. And he quickly provided her with her cosmetics and her portion of food, and with seven chosen young women from the king’s palace, and advanced her and her young women to the best place in the harem.

Then his wife Zeresh and all his friends said to him, “Let a gallows fifty cubits high be made, and in the morning tell the king to have Mordecai hanged upon it. Then go joyfully with the king to the feast.” This idea pleased Haman, and he had the gallows made.
“They wrong the barren, childless woman,
Sing to him a new song;
The words of his mouth are trouble and deceit;
For though, while he lives, he counts himself blessed—
Do good to Zion in your good pleasure;
This will please the LORD more than an ox
You are good and do good;
Do good, O LORD, to those who are good,
The tongue of the wise commends knowledge,
A glad heart makes a cheerful face,
A joyful heart is good medicine,
Three things are stately in their tread;
Sorrow is better than laughter,

Rejoice, O young man, in your youth, and let your heart cheer you in the days of your youth. Walk in the ways of your heart and the sight of your eyes. But know that for all these things God will bring you into judgment.
learn to do good;
“Take a harp;
Tell us what is to come hereafter,
Then the LORD said to me, “You have seen well, for I am watching over my word to perform it.”
“How well you direct your course
“For my people are foolish;
Thus says the LORD of hosts, the God of Israel: Amend your ways and your deeds, and I will let you dwell in this place.
“For if you truly amend your ways and your deeds, if you truly execute justice one with another,
But this command I gave them: ‘Obey my voice, and I will be your God, and you shall be my people. And walk in all the way that I command you, that it may be well with you.’
Their idols are like scarecrows in a cucumber field,
Can the Ethiopian change his skin
and if it does evil in my sight, not listening to my voice, then I will relent of the good that I had intended to do to it.

Now, therefore, say to the men of Judah and the inhabitants of Jerusalem: ‘Thus says the LORD, Behold, I am shaping disaster against you and devising a plan against you. Return, every one from his evil way, and amend your ways and your deeds.’
Now therefore mend your ways and your deeds, and obey the voice of the LORD your God, and the LORD will relent of the disaster that he has pronounced against you.
I will make with them an everlasting covenant, that I will not turn away from doing good to them. And I will put the fear of me in their hearts, that they may not turn from me.
I will rejoice in doing them good, and I will plant them in this land in faithfulness, with all my heart and all my soul.

I have sent to you all my servants the prophets, sending them persistently, saying, ‘Turn now every one of you from his evil way, and amend your deeds, and do not go after other gods to serve them, and then you shall dwell in the land that I gave to you and your fathers.’ But you did not incline your ear or listen to me.
Jeremiah said, “You shall not be given to them. Obey now the voice of the LORD in what I say to you, and it shall be well with you, and your life shall be spared.

Gedaliah the son of Ahikam, son of Shaphan, swore to them and their men, saying, “Do not be afraid to serve the Chaldeans. Dwell in the land and serve the king of Babylon, and it shall be well with you.
Whether it is good or bad, we will obey the voice of the LORD our God to whom we are sending you, that it may be well with us when we obey the voice of the LORD our God.”
And behold, you are to them like one who sings lustful songs with a beautiful voice and plays well on an instrument, for they hear what you say, but they will not do it.

And I will multiply on you man and beast, and they shall multiply and be fruitful. And I will cause you to be inhabited as in your former times, and will do more good to you than ever before. Then you will know that I am the LORD.

Then the king was exceedingly glad, and commanded that Daniel be taken up out of the den. So Daniel was taken up out of the den, and no kind of harm was found on him, because he had trusted in his God.
Israel is a luxuriant vine
And the LORD said, “Do you do well to be angry?”
But God said to Jonah, “Do you do well to be angry for the plant?” And he said, “Yes, I do well to be angry, angry enough to die.”
Should this be said, O house of Jacob?
Their hands are on what is evil, to do it well;
Are you better than Thebes
At that time I will search Jerusalem with lamps,
so again have I purposed in these days to bring good to Jerusalem and to the house of Judah; fear not.
And I will say to my soul, “Soul, you have ample goods laid up for many years; relax, eat, drink, be merry.”’
And bring the fattened calf and kill it, and let us eat and celebrate.
For this my son was dead, and is alive again; he was lost, and is found.’ And they began to celebrate.
but he answered his father, ‘Look, these many years I have served you, and I never disobeyed your command, yet you never gave me a young goat, that I might celebrate with my friends.
It was fitting to celebrate and be glad, for this your brother was dead, and is alive; he was lost, and is found.’”
“There was a rich man who was clothed in purple and fine linen and who feasted sumptuously every day.
therefore my heart was glad, and my tongue rejoiced;my flesh also will dwell in hope.
And they made a calf in those days, and offered a sacrifice to the idol and were rejoicing in the works of their hands.
And again it says, “Rejoice, O Gentiles, with his people.”

For it is written, “Rejoice, O barren one who does not bear;break forth and cry aloud, you who are not in labor!For the children of the desolate one will be morethan those of the one who has a husband.”
and those who dwell on the earth will rejoice over them and make merry and exchange presents, because these two prophets had been a torment to those who dwell on the earth.
Therefore, rejoice, O heavens and you who dwell in them! But woe to you, O earth and sea, for the devil has come down to you in great wrath, because he knows that his time is short!”
Rejoice over her, O heaven,and you saints and apostles and prophets,for God has given judgment for you against her!”
And Jethro rejoiced for all the good that the Lord had done to Israel, in that he had delivered them out of the hand of the Egyptians.
That night—let thick darkness seize it!Let it not rejoice among the days of the year;let it not come into the number of the months.
For you make him most blessed forever; you make him glad with the joy of your presence.
They had gone only a short distance from the city. Now Joseph said to his steward, “Up, follow after the men, and when you overtake them, say to them, ‘Why have you repaid evil for good?
As for you, you meant evil against me, but God meant it for good, to bring it about that many people should be kept alive, as they are today.
And Jethro rejoiced for all the good that the LORD had done to Israel, in that he had delivered them out of the hand of the Egyptians.
‘If Balak should give me his house full of silver and gold, I would not be able to go beyond the word of the LORD, to do either good or bad of my own will. What the LORD speaks, that will I speak’?
You shall not seek their peace or their prosperity all your days forever.

And the LORD will make you abound in prosperity, in the fruit of your womb and in the fruit of your livestock and in the fruit of your ground, within the land that the LORD swore to your fathers to give you.

The LORD your God will make you abundantly prosperous in all the work of your hand, in the fruit of your womb and in the fruit of your cattle and in the fruit of your ground. For the LORD will again take delight in prospering you, as he took delight in your fathers,
and they did not show steadfast love to the family of Jerubbaal (that is, Gideon) in return for all the good that he had done to Israel.

For I was ashamed to ask the king for a band of soldiers and horsemen to protect us against the enemy on our way, since we had told the king, “The hand of our God is for good on all who seek him, and the power of his wrath is against all who forsake him.”

Therefore do not give your daughters to their sons, neither take their daughters for your sons, and never seek their peace or prosperity, that you may be strong and eat the good of the land and leave it for an inheritance to your children forever.’
But when Sanballat the Horonite and Tobiah the Ammonite servant heard this, it displeased them greatly that someone had come to seek the welfare of the people of Israel.

And I told them of the hand of my God that had been upon me for good, and also of the words that the king had spoken to me. And they said, “Let us rise up and build.” So they strengthened their hands for the good work.
Remember for my good, O my God, all that I have done for this people.
Also they spoke of his good deeds in my presence and reported my words to him. And Tobiah sent letters to make me afraid.
and I provided for the wood offering at appointed times, and for the firstfruits.
“My days are swifter than a runner;
Another dies in bitterness of soul,
“Agree with God, and be at peace;
I say to the LORD, “You are my Lord;
They repay me evil for good;
Those who render me evil for good
You crown the year with your bounty;
your flock found a dwelling in it;
Show me a sign of your favor,
that I may look upon the prosperity of your chosen ones,
So they reward me evil for good,
If anyone returns evil for good,

one person who has no other, either son or brother, yet there is no end to all his toil, and his eyes are never satisfied with riches, so that he never asks, “For whom am I toiling and depriving myself of pleasure?” This also is vanity and an unhappy business.
When goods increase, they increase who eat them, and what advantage has their owner but to see them with his eyes?

Behold, what I have seen to be good and fitting is to eat and drink and find enjoyment in all the toil with which one toils under the sun the few days of his life that God has given him, for this is his lot.

If a man fathers a hundred children and lives many years, so that the days of his years are many, but his soul is not satisfied with life’s good things, and he also has no burial, I say that a stillborn child is better off than he.
Even though he should live a thousand years twice over, yet enjoy no good—do not all go to the one place?
In the day of prosperity be joyful, and in the day of adversity consider: God has made the one as well as the other, so that man may not find out anything that will be after him.
Wisdom is better than weapons of war, but one sinner destroys much good.
For even your brothers and the house of your father,
The LORD said to me: “Do not pray for the welfare of this people.
and if it does evil in my sight, not listening to my voice, then I will relent of the good that I had intended to do to it.
Should good be repaid with evil?
For I have set my face against this city for harm and not for good, declares the LORD: it shall be given into the hand of the king of Babylon, and he shall burn it with fire.’
“Thus says the LORD, the God of Israel: Like these good figs, so I will regard as good the exiles from Judah, whom I have sent away from this place to the land of the Chaldeans.
I will set my eyes on them for good, and I will bring them back to this land. I will build them up, and not tear them down; I will plant them, and not pluck them up.
“For thus says the LORD: Just as I have brought all this great disaster upon this people, so I will bring upon them all the good that I promise them.

And this city shall be to me a name of joy, a praise and a glory before all the nations of the earth who shall hear of all the good that I do for them. They shall fear and tremble because of all the good and all the prosperity I provide for it.

“Go, and say to Ebed-melech the Ethiopian, ‘Thus says the LORD of hosts, the God of Israel: Behold, I will fulfill my words against this city for harm and not for good, and they shall be accomplished before you on that day.
Behold, I am watching over them for disaster and not for good. All the men of Judah who are in the land of Egypt shall be consumed by the sword and by famine, until there is an end of them.
And he spoke kindly to him and gave him a seat above the seats of the kings who were with him in Babylon.

rows.description: G9320 suneufrainomai: STEP confirms no verses available.
te.ev (H2868) is Aramaic equivalent of ya.tav (H3190); shares same verse set. No independent verse records produced.
eupsucheō (G2174): only 1 NT occurrence (Phil 2:19). Thin data.
cha.dah (H2302): only 3 occurrences (Exo 18:9; Job 3:6; Psa 21:6). Thin data.

rows.note: Primary tov-root; ya.tav is the verbal form. Shared with to.vah (H2896C, this registry) and XREF tov (H2895, reg.103) and tuv (H2898, reg.103).
Aramaic form of the tov-root; cognate of ya.tav (H3190). Same root family, Aramaic register.
Nominal tov-root: welfare, prosperity, good things. Shares root with ya.tav (H3190) and XREF tov (H2895) and tuv (H2898).
Verbal chd-root; shared with XREF ched.vah (H2304, reg.097 joy Part1). Verb-noun pair spanning registries 186 and 097.
Compound eu + psuchē: gladness expressed as a well-souled state. Related words are all psuchē compounds.
eu + phrēn root. Verbal form; shares root with XREF eufrosunē (G2167, reg.097 joy Part3) and with suneufrainomai (G9320, this registry).
Compound sun + eufrainō: to rejoice together with. Same eufr-root as eufrainō (G2165). No verses available.

rows.relationship_note: Root component of eupsucheō; gladness expressed as a well-souled state
Nominal form of the eufr-root; XREF registry 097 joy Part3
Root component phrēn in eu-phrain-ō; gladness as right-mindedness
Nominal form of the chd-root; XREF registry 097 joy Part1
Core adjectival/verbal form of the tov-root; XREF registry 103 love Part2
Abstract nominal form of the tov-root; XREF registry 103 love Part2
